title: Export Command (Project Menu)
description:
The Project menu Export Command uses a wizard to import projects to EPSON RC+ 6.0 projects.

When a project is exported, the files from the source are copied to a new project folder, so the original project is not changed.

Note:  SPEL+ commands and syntax added to the EPSON RC+ 7.0 are not supported by EPSON RC+ 6.0. It is recommended to change the compiler version according to the version of your controller and check the compatibility before exporting projects.

Exporting a PC project

Follow these steps to export a project:

1.  Select EPSON RC+ 7.0 menu-[Project]-[Export] to display the [Export Project] dialog.

2.  Select a drive. A project list will be refreshed and exportable projects will be displayed. Select a project you want to export from the list and click <Next> button.

3.  Name of a new project is set to the name of the exported project. The name of the new project can be changed. Select the destination drive and project folder. Then, click <Next>.

4.  Confirm the export source and destination.

5.  Click <Export>. If the destination already exists, you will be asked whether or not to overwrite the project.
